steps.title: Use the graph to eliminate quickly
steps.content: All four choices have y-intercept $-1$, so do not spend time on the intercept. The graph falls from left to right, so the slope must be negative. That eliminates A and B immediately.

steps.title: Use the two marked points
steps.content: The marked points are $(0,-1)$ and $(8,-6)$. Put those into a Desmos table and run $y_1\sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-\frac{5}{8}$ and $b=-1$, so the equation is $f(x)=-\frac{5}{8}x-1$.

steps.title: Confirm with Desmos or the choices
steps.content: In Desmos, graph the four answer-choice lines and compare them with the image; only choice C goes through both marked points. Choice D has the right negative sign, but its slope $-\frac{8}{5}$ is too steep. Therefore choice C, $f(x)=-\frac{5}{8}x-1$, is correct.
